description: Self-heal jarvis_heartbeat: In `core.py` line 317, guard with `char_count = len(result.stdout or "")` or ensure the subprocess call passes `stdout=subprocess.PIPE`.
goal_context: Automated self-heal: jarvis_heartbeat runner failed. Root cause: `collect_hook_output_size` calls `len(result.stdout)` but `result.stdout` is `None` because `subprocess.run` was called without `capture_output=True` or `stdout=PIPE`, so stdout was not captured.. Diagnosis log: memory\learning\failures\2026-04-16_self-diagnose-jarvis-heartbeat-2.md
isc: jarvis_heartbeat exits cleanly after fix | Verify: python tools/scripts/jarvis_heartbeat.py --test
notes: Auto-generated by self-diagnose-wrapper L1. Category: code_bug

description: Self-heal jarvis_heartbeat: In collectors/core.py run_collector (or the subprocess invocation feeding collect_hook_output_size), decode subprocess output with encoding="utf-8" and errors="replace", and guard char_count = len(result.stdout or "").
goal_context: Automated self-heal: jarvis_heartbeat runner failed. Root cause: A subprocess reader thread crashed with UnicodeDecodeError decoding cp1252, leaving result.stdout as None, which then caused collect_hook_output_size to call len() on None..
